description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the node:11 Docker image with a cache for node_modules/ and yarn.lock, and defines the stages install, analyze, test, pre-build, build, and deploy. In the install stage add an install job that runs `yarn`, creates `coverage` and `.nyc_output` directories, and stores node_modules, coverage, and .nyc_output as artifacts for 1 hour. In the analyze stage add a lint job that depends on install, runs only on the develop branch, and executes `yarn lint` using node:11. In the pre-build stage add a pre-build job that runs on develop and master, replaces `%jwtsecret%` in src/config.ts with the `$JWT_SECRET_DEV` variable via `sed -i -e 's/%jwtsecret%/'"$JWT_SECRET_DEV"'/g' src/config.ts`, and saves src/config.ts as a 1‑hour artifact. In the test stage add a unit test job that depends on install, runs `yarn test:coverage`, and archives node_modules, coverage, and .nyc_output as artifacts for 1 hour. In the build stage add a build job that depends on pre-build, runs on develop and master, executes `yarn` then `yarn build`, publishes the build directory as a 1‑day artifact, and in the deploy stage add dev:deploy and prod:deploy jobs (both depending on build, using the aws-base image, running only on develop or master respectively) that sync the build folder to their respective S3 buckets with `aws s3 sync ./build/ s3://... --acl public-read --delete`.
